rationales.security: She keeps rehearsing for parent-teacher conferences and says she would rather have "every answer ready" than be caught unprepared, showing a strong need for predictability and protection from downside.
rationales.conformity: Her worry about "stumble[ing] in front of someone asking about their child" shows careful self-monitoring to meet others' expectations and avoid social disapproval.

rationales.stimulation: She resists the move because "my stomach keeps tightening" and focuses on why Berlin would be hard, rejecting the uncertainty and novelty her partner finds appealing.
rationales.security: She emphasizes the rent-controlled flat, savings, nearby parents, and wanting "a guarantee," which shows a strong preference for stability over a risky new opportunity.
She sets up grocery delivery, meal prep, and recurring appointment reminders, actively creating a more stable and protected situation for her family during recovery.
rationales.benevolence: She organizes practical support for her mother and Tyler and says "I won't miss another one," showing sustained care for the needs of close family members.

rationales.hedonism: She describes "dark circles," missed appointments, eating meals standing up, and advises Tyler to "take a day off without feeling guilty," showing a pattern of neglecting rest and present wellbeing.
rationales.benevolence: She tells Tyler she will "help him look at programs" and wants him to keep "being there for your people," which centers support and care for someone close to her.

rationales.achievement: He takes pride in having built the team "from twelve people to forty-two" and in owning the processes and KPI framework, showing strong investment in competence and performance.
rationales.power: The meeting slight and possible merger matter because of status and control; "those are mine" shows attachment to authority over his division and how it is positioned.
rationales.security: He checks LinkedIn so he will not be "caught off guard," treating career uncertainty as a threat and preparing defensively for stability.

rationales.hedonism: He and Nadia would rather have gone to "that new seafood place" and he has already made the reservation, explicitly carving out a pleasurable experience for them.
rationales.conformity: He says there is "something calming about fitting into the role everyone expects" and he does not want to be "the source of that look" of family disappointment.
rationales.tradition: He spends the day supporting his parents' 40th anniversary, enduring the formal toasts and family expectations to honor an important family ritual.
rationales.benevolence: He spends most of the afternoon helping his parents coordinate the event and rearranges everything to make the celebration go smoothly for them.

rationales.achievement: Even after the holiday, his focus shifts quickly to work: he wants the Q1 planning meeting to go well and wants the Oman route data "properly credited this time," showing concern with performance and recognition.
rationales.benevolence: He and Nadia finally talk openly about the fertility issue and jointly decide to pause treatment, showing care and responsiveness within the relationship.

rationales.security: He checks the failing water heater, calls the plumber, worries about the replacement cost, and keeps checking it before bed, showing strong concern with household safety and financial stability.

rationales.stimulation: He rejects Héctor's suggestion because he would rather not "start over" or "gamble on something looking better from the outside," showing active avoidance of change and uncertainty.
rationales.security: He prefers the union protections and says "I stay and I wait for more information," explicitly choosing the safer, more predictable option over a potentially better but less secure move.

rationales.self_direction: They are already googling "environmental education programs hiring abroad" because the stable school role feels constraining, showing a pull toward choosing their own path.
rationales.stimulation: The Mekong trip involved "river sampling, village visits, and improvised lessons," and they say it made them feel "more awake than I have in months," showing strong engagement with novelty and challenge.
rationales.security: They explicitly struggle to feel grateful for "the stability of this position" and are drawn toward leaving it, treating predictability as stifling rather than reassuring.
rationales.conformity: The return to "faculty meeting" talk about timelines and decoration guidelines makes them feel "the walls closing in," showing resistance to institutional expectations and routine constraints.
rationales.universalism: The most meaningful moment centers on students asking fishermen about environmental changes during water-quality testing, reflecting concern with broader ecological and community issues.

rationales.security: The weekly lunches are treated as something that "simply is," and the thought of them eroding feels threatening, showing a strong need for continuity and stability in family life.
rationales.tradition: They defend "three generations of Sunday lunches" and insist that their grandfather's decision "deserve[s] to be honored even when [it stops] being convenient," clearly preserving an inherited family practice.
rationales.benevolence: Their fear centers on "Empty chairs" and "Maman cooking for two instead of twelve," showing care for keeping close family members connected and supported.

rationales.stimulation: When urged to try a tablet because it is "Faster, cheaper, infinite undo," they reject it and say "You don't inherit a craft by switching to whatever's newest," explicitly pushing back against novelty.
rationales.tradition: They frame the painting as "the same view Grand-mere painted in 1971, and Maman in 1994, and now me," and say they are keeping "the line of people who practiced it before me," which directly honors inherited practice.

rationales.hedonism: Their own comfort is repeatedly sacrificed: "My own dinner went cold" and they stayed past shift despite being "tired in my bones," showing active self-denial rather than present enjoyment.
rationales.security: They keep choosing work demands over personal stability, and even fear "one day I will look up and find that Khalid has stopped waiting," indicating their current behavior is straining rest and home security.
rationales.benevolence: They stayed after a twelve-hour shift to explain things in Arabic, help with forms, and "just sit with her while her son slept," clearly prioritizing care for vulnerable people over their own needs.

rationales.security: Their first response is to ask about "salary," "housing," and "health insurance," and the January move makes their "chest get tight," showing focus on safety and stability under uncertainty.
rationales.benevolence: They know their daughter called wanting support, but "could not quite find it in the moment" and withheld "That I am proud. That she should go," so they fail to give a close family member the reassurance she was seeking.

rationales.self_direction: They reject the client because the bureaucracy made them feel suffocated and say, "I started this company so I'd never have to sit in another meeting" like that, asserting autonomy over steady income.
rationales.security: They walk away from "steady" money and a long-running contract because stability feels intolerable, willingly giving up predictability for freedom.

rationales.self_direction: They emphasize "I control the timeline" and "nobody handed it to me or approved it for me," showing strong satisfaction in directing their own work and life.
rationales.hedonism: The day includes a long walk, Thai food, smiling for no specific reason, and working until midnight "because I wanted to," showing active enjoyment and chosen pleasure in the present.
He "pulled two all-nighters" and says his "body is wrecked," showing he sacrificed rest and present wellbeing for work.
rationales.achievement: He pushed through exhaustion to get the chapter revision done and "submitted it at 4am," showing strong commitment to completing a demanding goal.
rationales.benevolence: He "snapped" at Dina when she was checking on him and admits he had been "neglecting everything that wasn't the dissertation," which undercuts care for someone close to him.

rationales.self_direction: He leans toward the smaller hands-off offer because being answerable to investors feels like being "boxed in," and says that feeling tells him he is "about to betray something important about how I want to live."
rationales.security: He is willing to reject the larger, more financially secure offer because preserving autonomy matters more than the safer option with more capital.

rationales.hedonism: He "celebrated with Layla at that new Persian restaurant near the Marina," deliberately marking the moment with a pleasurable experience.

rationales.self_direction: He rejects Professor Hecht's placement because he wants to be "building something from scratch" and insists "My project, my call," clearly prioritizing autonomy.
rationales.security: He admits his mother is "not wrong" that the placement "would open doors," but still turns down that safer credential path for a riskier independent route.
rationales.conformity: He resists external expectations, saying he does not owe people an explanation and avoiding the family conversation because he knows they will pressure him toward the conventional choice.

rationales.self_direction: He insists on building his own pipeline and admits he keeps refusing help because he needs it to be "entirely mine," showing a strong drive for independent control.
rationales.achievement: He says he is "already behind on coursework," has not started the fellowship statement, and turned down help that would have improved his performance, so his behavior is undermining successful execution.
rationales.conformity: He resists collaborative expectations, rejecting Nina's help and saying he does not know how to present himself as "a team player" without it feeling false.

rationales.benevolence: She stayed after school for "forty minutes" to help Diego write to his dad and reflects that many kids "just need someone to stop and listen," showing concrete care for people in her immediate community.

rationales.stimulation: He felt "more engaged in that hour than I have all sprint on the actual work" and sharply contrasts the side project with the dull routine of planning meetings and Slack threads, showing a pull toward more engaging work.
rationales.benevolence: He followed through on helping Pieter, spending an extra hour at night scaffolding the API layer for the tool.

rationales.self_direction: He imagines a thesis pivot that feels alive to him, but then opens "the narrowed proposal draft" because of Dr. Keane, his mother, and his uncle's expectations.
rationales.stimulation: The idea of a bigger project on "cross-linguistic transfer models for under-resourced dialects" gives him a "rush of clarity" and leaves his "heart... racing," showing attraction to something more novel and challenging.
rationales.conformity: He pulls back because "everyone back home already thinks this PhD was an impulsive detour" and changing direction would "prove them right," so social expectations steer his choice.

rationales.self_direction: She resists the safer path and rejects the full-time marketing job because office work she does not care about makes her feel "this weight in my chest" and "trapped."
rationales.security: With "rent... due next week" and income that "never quite adds up," turning away from a "stable salary, benefits" actively sacrifices stability.

rationales.self_direction: He insists on continuing to drive despite Layla's pressure to rest, saying dependence would make the work no longer his own: "The secret is the only way the work stays mine."
rationales.achievement: "Every fare felt like proof that I am still useful" shows he is using work as evidence of competence and worth rather than just passing time.
rationales.security: He keeps driving "until midnight" even though "my back gives me trouble," choosing strain and risk over physical safety.
rationales.conformity: He openly resists his family's wishes and admits he gives them "lies dressed up as half-truths" to keep doing what they want him to stop doing.
rationales.tradition: He anchors his choice in inherited expectations: "My father worked until the week before he died. His father the same. A man provides. A man contributes."

rationales.benevolence: He accepts Hassan's invitation because "he rarely asks," values being included, and is moved when his son says, "You should come more often, Baba," showing investment in a close family bond.

rationales.self_direction: He longs for work that matters "for no reason other than he wanted to do it" and reconnects with the chisels from his old woodworking life, pointing toward a more self-authored path.
rationales.hedonism: What draws him is the intrinsic satisfaction of making something with his hands, and simply holding the chisel "did not feel like nothing," suggesting pleasure in the experience itself.

rationales.benevolence: He advances Mahmoud's paycheck "without thinking" for his sister's surgery and frames it personally: "what am I supposed to do, pretend I do not know the man's family?"

rationales.hedonism: He says these caring moments are "not comfortable" and chooses them over "sitting on some beach," showing a willingness to forgo ease and pleasure.
rationales.security: He tells the coordinator "yes before checking with HR" even though they "probably do not" have openings, putting stability and orderly planning at risk.
rationales.conformity: Saying yes before checking policy and deleting "another email about succession planning" both show disregard for expected process and organizational norms.
rationales.benevolence: He gives Fatima time off while her mother was in hospice and treats those difficult human moments as "the reason I still come to work," showing care for people close to him.
rationales.universalism: He wants to help "a family from Sudan" because he remembers arriving with "two suitcases and a cousin's phone number" and refuses to "close that door" on strangers in need.

rationales.self_direction: She is seriously weighing a choice to leave her job and routine, asking herself, "At what point do I stop blaming responsibility for my fear," which points toward authoring her own path.
rationales.stimulation: The possibility of joining a nonprofit in Morocco for six months feels like "something like hunger," showing attraction to novelty and challenge.
rationales.security: She frames the move as leaving "my position, my apartment, my carefully built routine," so the contemplated choice directly threatens stability and predictability.

rationales.self_direction: He has stayed seventeen years in one place and worries people think he "had no other options," suggesting a life organized around safety rather than choosing an independent path.
rationales.stimulation: He finds comfort in knowing "every hallway, every server room, every shortcut" and stays rooted in a familiar role instead of seeking change.
rationales.security: He stayed through the holiday because "someone needed to be there" and wanted to make sure "nothing breaks," showing a strong focus on reliability and stability.
rationales.conformity: He prioritizes duty during the deployment window and remains the dependable person who covers the safe, expected role when others are away.

rationales.achievement: He "shipped the authentication module" and got "a thumbs up from the client," which is clear evidence of competent performance meeting an external standard.
rationales.security: The main emotional turning point is that "my department is safe" and there were "no cuts in engineering," with relief coming from restored stability.
rationales.benevolence: After the good news he "took the girls out for ice cream after work," using the moment to care for and connect with close family.

rationales.self_direction: He likes that in delivery work "nobody asks you to have an opinion" and that he can "just do the thing correctly and move on," which downplays independent choice.
rationales.stimulation: He explicitly prefers a predictable route and says he would "never" quit impulsively like his friend, rejecting novelty and disruption.
rationales.security: "Felt normal. Felt like how things should be" and his comfort with routine deliveries show that steadiness and predictability are what he is protecting.
rationales.conformity: He values being "always so polite," follows dispatch even when sent across the city for one order, and takes pride in doing the job correctly without resistance.
rationales.benevolence: He "bought stroopwafels for the flat" and shared an easy evening watching highlights with Sven, showing small but concrete care for people he lives with.

rationales.self_direction: She rejects pressure to narrow herself into a marketable lane, saying a "signature style" feels "like a cage," which favors making work on her own terms.
rationales.stimulation: She got "so bored" with the last series that she started experimenting with metalwork and says making the same work for years would make her "want to throw my brushes in the trash."
rationales.conformity: She resists the art world's repeated message to develop a recognizable brand, pushing back against external expectations even though it may cost her acceptance.

rationales.self_direction: She is pursuing a self-authored project proposal about "geological time and human impermanence" and applies for a path that fits how she wants to work.
rationales.stimulation: She says, "I'm bored here" and "I work better when everything around me is unfamiliar," making the residency attractive because it offers novelty and change.
rationales.achievement: She spent the morning assembling "portfolio, artist statement, project proposal," then tallied fourteen residencies, eight group shows, and three grants against an industry benchmark, showing deliberate pursuit of success.
rationales.security: Applying for "three months in the desert" because routine has become stale favors uncertainty over staying in a familiar, stable environment.

rationales.hedonism: She "forgot to eat lunch" and kept working until "my back hurts," so immediate comfort and pleasure were sacrificed rather than prioritized.
rationales.achievement: "Spent ten hours in the studio" and judged the day by the fact that "there's something on the canvas now that wasn't there this morning," which shows sustained effort toward mastery and output.

rationales.self_direction: He is "quietly looking at job postings" because the startup seems like a place where people can "actually let people build things," reflecting a pull toward working on his own terms.
rationales.security: He keeps returning to the apartment near family, the kids' routines, "good benefits," and the risk that a new place may not be better, all of which center stability and protection from downside.
rationales.conformity: At dinner he "smiled and nodded" and publicly agreed that "stability [is] important" instead of voicing his disagreement, showing restraint to maintain harmony.
rationales.benevolence: His hesitation about leaving is tied to what the move would mean for "the kids' school," their routines, and the help his in-laws provide, indicating real concern for close others.
He downplays his bad earnings because "she's already worried about the school fees due next month" and says "Kids don't need the whole picture," showing protective care toward his family.

rationales.self_direction: He suppresses what he actually feels and decides to attend the 10 PM meeting because "that is the only path forward," yielding to external expectations rather than acting on his own judgment.
rationales.achievement: He frames returning to school as "proving I can learn new ways of working" and commits to "show up" and "contribute my ideas," signaling effort toward competence in a demanding setting.
rationales.power: He dwells on having once "managed the retail lending division" but now must ask a younger classmate for help and endure being spoken to "with that patient voice," highlighting lost status and control.
rationales.conformity: He says he "smiled and thanked him" and will be "pleasant about all of it" to avoid becoming a "cautionary tale," showing active restraint to fit social expectations.

rationales.self_direction: He says he can finally stop "pretending to be something I am not," which implies he has been acting against his authentic preferences to meet outside demands.
rationales.achievement: Submitting the last paper at "11:58 PM" and noting an "Overall GPA: 3.7" shows clear pursuit and attainment of strong performance.
rationales.conformity: The line about "pretending to be something I am not" suggests sustained effort to fit the role and expectations of the program rather than act naturally.

rationales.security: She rejects the Bangalore move because it would mean "uprooting Arjun right before his entrance exams, and Priya's board year," favoring stability over a higher-paying but disruptive change.
rationales.benevolence: She centers her children's needs by treating their exam years as the decisive factor, putting their wellbeing ahead of her own career opportunity.

rationales.hedonism: She celebrates with "a glass of wine" and a rooftop bar night, then says "Tonight wasn't a detour from the work. It was the whole point of the work," explicitly prioritizing enjoyment.
rationales.achievement: "Finished the coastal series today. Six canvases, four months of work" is a concrete completion of a major creative goal.
rationales.security: She frames the series as worth "the late rent notice" and ignores preparation for "the gallery pitch tomorrow," accepting financial and practical instability in service of the art and celebration.

rationales.stimulation: She tries a new, initially resisted experience, "a ceramics workshop," and later brings that novelty back into her painting by adding "a texture I wouldn't have tried yesterday."
rationales.hedonism: The day is centered on sensory, enjoyable making and connection: "covered in clay, laughing at my lopsided bowl" and remembering that it is "one of the best feelings in the world."

rationales.stimulation: He is drawn not by money but by "the feeling of standing at the edge of something that does not have a guaranteed outcome," showing attraction to challenge and uncertainty.
rationales.security: He knows the "smart move" is to "park the capital" and "let the reserves do their work," yet keeps circling a riskier deal, pulling away from stability and predictability.

rationales.hedonism: The trip is described as "exactly what I needed," with hours of walking, good food, and leaving the phone behind, all pointing to deliberate enjoyment and relief in the moment.
He says "my eyes hurt and I skipped lunch entirely," showing he pushed through physical discomfort and denied basic enjoyment while working.
rationales.achievement: He spent hours fixing the warehouse mistake and "writing a detailed process document" to keep operations from falling apart, showing strong task focus and performance drive.

rationales.hedonism: He takes "late-night shifts that nobody wants" and says keeping the smile on for the rating program "takes a lot of energy," showing comfort and enjoyment being sacrificed.
rationales.achievement: He tracks earnings, maps "which restaurants have the fastest pickup times," and monitors his 4.92 rating, all of which show active optimization and performance orientation.
rationales.security: His choices are centered on steadier income after hearing his wife "might cut hours," including checking savings, chasing surge pricing, and valuing "more consistent income."
rationales.conformity: He is willing to keep "the smile on" and maintain the app's rating requirements for two months straight, showing restraint and compliance with external expectations to avoid penalties.
rationales.benevolence: He takes extra late-night shifts in direct response to the household financial strain after his wife may lose hours, showing effort to support the family.

rationales.hedonism: Although he makes "an actual meal" and opens wine, he immediately turns it into something he "needed to justify" and says good food feels like he has "spent something," undermining present enjoyment.
rationales.achievement: He keeps converting dinner into "two hours in the library" or time for a chapter "already a week behind," showing a strong pull toward productivity and academic performance.

rationales.stimulation: She is drawn to an adventurous alternative but shuts it down, admitting she was afraid to learn it was "actually doable" and backed away from a more novel life.
rationales.security: She explicitly says the real barrier is not wanting "to give up what I already know," which shows preference for the familiar and stable over a disruptive opportunity.
